body.title: cc_queue.py flush_session endpoint returns 404
body.result: Migrated flush_session + flush_decision from defunct /api/cc_notebook (removed in web-UI redesign 02ce529d) to the active /api/cc_send endpoint. Payload now a plain channel message with [FLUSH ...] prefix for parseable recognition. Verified live. Commit acba41eb.
body.description:
Observed twice during session 2026-04-18b when /savestateauto ran `cc_queue.py flush_session <session_id> <text>`.

## Symptom
```
  [Igor flush failed — Igor not running? HTTP Error 404: Not Found]
  (session logged locally only)
```

Igor IS running (other channel posts succeed). So it's not 'Igor not running' — it's that the endpoint doesn't exist on the live server (or moved).

## Impact
- Sessions are not being flushed to Igor's memory
- CC's working-session summary isn't reaching Igor's graph
- Local logging still works, so nothing is lost — but the cross-agent handoff path is broken

## First steps
1. Read lab/claudecode/cc_queue.py to find what URL flush_session hits
2. Check the utility closet / Igor web server for matching route
3. Likely: endpoint was renamed or moved during a recent UC refactor (e.g. T-uc-channel-migration era) and cc_queue.py wasn't updated
4. Fix is probably one-line — update the URL in cc_queue.py, or restore the endpoint on the server side if it was dropped

## Related
- Several recent utility-closet refactors touched comms/routing — any of those may have dropped this endpoint
